article_title: Global Renewable Energy Capacity Reaches 49 Percent as 2025 Additions Hit Record 692 GW, IRENA Reports

content_preview.title: Global Renewable Energy Capacity Reaches 49 Percent as 2025 Additions Hit Record 692 GW, IRENA Reports
content_preview.summary: The International Renewable Energy Agency's latest data shows the world added a record 692 gigawatts of renewable power capacity in 2025, bringing renewables to 49 percent of total global installed capacity and within reach of the halfway mark for the first time.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Renewable energy now accounts for [49 percent of the world's total installed power capacity](https://www.irena.org/News/pressreleases/2026/Apr/Near-700-GW-Surge-in-2025-Proves-Renewable-Energy-Resilience), up from 46.3 percent a year earlier, according to data released by the International Renewable Energy Agency on April 1, 2026. The milestone came after the world added a record [692 gigawatts of renewable capacity in 2025](https://www.irena.org/News/pressreleases/2026/Apr/Near-700...

editor_notes.content_quality: Comprehensive renewable energy data article with IRENA statistics, regional breakdowns, and geopolitical context.
editor_notes.source_verification: 3 sources checked. IRENA: HTTP 403 but Fortune source snapshot corroborates all IRENA statistics (692 GW, 49%, La Camera quote). Fortune: captured in source snapshot — confirms 85.6% of new capacity, La Camera 'decentralized resilient' quote, Spain 60% solar/wind. Electrek: VERIFIED — confirms 55.2 GW US renewable, 36.6% capacity, 2026 projections (41.6 GW solar, 14 GW wind, 22.7 GW storage, -4.3 GW fossil).
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All statistics verified across corroborating sources. Regional breakdowns, solar/wind splits, and US data confirmed.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral. Data-driven with appropriate context. La Camera quote is from official IRENA press release.
editor_notes.originality: No prior IRENA 2025 renewable capacity report coverage. New topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong energy data article. All claims corroborated despite IRENA 403. Approved.
